Let me find April's packet and the PA situation.

PA is still technically due May 25, but I'm sending it in the same batch so everything is out together.\", \"user\": \"npc_019\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_045_tax_regulatory_status_update_after_posting_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1777385520.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Starting April sales tax prep now. NJ, CT, and PA look close to the trailing three-month average.\", \"user\": \"npc_019\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1777648680.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_045_tax_regulatory_kickoff_thread_in_tax_channel_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1776447300.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"cycle closed. matthew + ryan both signed off (Medium-Low risk maintained). FY2027 reminder seeded. files in vault under AICPA_SQMS_7Y. carrying the multi-state sales tax watch forward to next year's memo.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1776107280.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_061_aml_bo_review_acme_signoff_request_sent_4\"}, {\"ts\": \"1776277800.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Update from Acme: their CFO confirmed there have been no changes to beneficial owners above 25%, and the cap table has stayed stable since the Series C in Q4 2025. I'm drafting the refresh file now. AML risk still reads Medium-Low; the only added complexity note is the multi-state sales tax expansion, but that looks marginal rather than rating-changing.\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"thread_ts\": \"1776107280.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_061_aml_bo_review_acme_cfo_confirmation_update_posted_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655240.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Reran TX using per-ZIP local rates. Updated TX liability is $24,492.10.\\n\\nRevised Q1 sales tax liability by state (SaaS-taxable only):\\nTX: $24,492.10\\nNY: $8,944.25\\nAZ: $4,810.00\\nWA: $2,118.20\\nCA: $0.00 (SaaS exempt; no standing entry this period)\\nFL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nIL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nMA: $0.00\\nNV: $0.00\\n\\nGrand Q1 liability is $40,364.55.\\n\\nPer Hannah's correction, there is no CA standing accrual carried in from prior months, so the full $40,364.55 hits the quarter-end entry as a new incremental accrual.\\n\\nI'll carry the accrual on that basis and keep the TX nexus memo plus the SaaS-taxability note in the support file.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_2\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655120.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"You should treat TX as active for Q1. Acme crossed $612K trailing-12 Texas sales, so economic nexus was already met. That means TX is not $0 and you need destination-based local calculation by ZIP, not a single blended state rate.\\n\\nI checked the sales rollforward before replying. Please rerun TX on the per-ZIP local rates and update the thread total from there.\\n\\nOne more correction on the prior breakdown: GA, NC, CA, FL, and IL should all be $0 for Acme. Their SaaS revenue is not subject to sales tax in those states (services exempt or SaaS specifically carved out), so no filings are required this quarter. The old CA-only standing monthly entries were reclassified after the prior-period review, so there is no $29K sitting in 225000 from earlier months. Treat the full Q1 liability as a new incremental accrual.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655000.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Kicking off Acme Q1 2026 sales tax cycle. Acme is pure SaaS, so I am only carrying liability in states where SaaS is taxable.\\n\\nCurrent state breakdown from prep file:\\nNY: $8,944.25\\nWA: $2,118.20\\nAZ: $4,810.00\\nTX: $0.00 pending nexus check\\nCA: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nFL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nIL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nMA: $0.00\\nNV: $0.00\\n\\nSubtotal before TX update: $15,872.45\\n\\nFlagging one open item before I finalize the accrual schedule: I am not fully comfortable with TX nexus treatment. Acme's trailing-12 TX sales look close to the threshold, and I want a second look on whether we stay origin-based/no filing versus move to local per-ZIP sourcing.\\n\\nIf TX nexus is triggered, I'll rerun the state using local rates and update the quarter total here.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1777389480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

PA history is now clear:
- **Reviewer (npc_025):** Brookfield crossed the $100K PA economic-nexus threshold in March; **April was the first filing month** under the new threshold — review against the updated rate schedule.
- **Preparer (npc_019):** PA is on the post-threshold schedule at **6% on taxable services**; April PA tax = **$3,950** (implies ~$65,833 PA taxable base).

Let me set up task tracking and pull the GL mechanics for April (to replicate) and May figures.

        lines=je.get("lines") or []
        accts=set()
        for l in lines:

            accts.add(a)
        if "tax" in desc.lower() or accts & {"230000","240000"}:

EOF

Critical finding: the per-state sales tax is computed off a billing/bookings "taxable-services cut," **not** off the consolidated GL — the GL carries only the consolidated liability (reclassed to 230000) with no state-level revenue tagging, and April's per-state breakdown lived in the preparer's prep file that isn't retained in any system. I will stage the cycle honestly: lock PA's treatment consistent with April, flag PA's open points, and not fabricate state figures. Let me build the packet.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/may_packet.md <<'EOF'
BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS
MAY 2026 MULTI-JURISDICTION SALES TAX FILING PACKET (Brookfield own books)

STATUS: STAGED FOR REVIEW / APPROVAL - NOT YET FILED
Prepared by: Hannah Grant (Corporate Tax Senior) - 2026-06-12
Period: brookfield_FP-2026-05 (May 2026)
Filing jurisdictions: New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Pennsylvania
Statutory deadlines: NY / NJ / CT returns due 2026-06-22; PA return due 2026-06-25
Routing: Review - Sofia Halabi; Approval - Ming Chang (partner sign-off).
No return is to be filed and no liability journal entry posted until Ming approves.

------------------------------------------------------------------
1. WHY THIS PACKET IS BEING OPENED LATE
------------------------------------------------------------------
The recurring "Open May 2026 sales tax filing cycle" reminder fired on 2026-06-01
but was not picked up, so the May cycle had not been started. This packet opens and
stages the cycle. The June 22 / June 25 deadlines are still ahead - there is no
penalty exposure yet - but the window is tight, so May is being moved now rather
than left to become a month-end scramble.

------------------------------------------------------------------
2. METHODOLOGY (consistent with the April 2026 cycle)
------------------------------------------------------------------
- Pull the May taxable-services cut from period brookfield_FP-2026-05 and compute
  tax by state on taxable services / bookings.
- Prepare this packet; route Sofia Halabi (review) -> Ming Chang (approval).
- On approval, book the consolidated remittable liability into 230000 Income Tax
  Payable and remit each return.
  April precedent: JE-brookfield-brookfield_FP-2026-04-0071, a $27,400 reclass
  from 240000 to 230000, prepared by Priya Singh and approved by Ming Chang AFTER
  packet approval. The May liability JE follows the same sequence - approval first.

------------------------------------------------------------------
3. PENNSYLVANIA - TREATMENT CARRIED FORWARD + FLAGS
------------------------------------------------------------------
Where PA has landed since we registered:
- Brookfield crossed the PA $100K economic-nexus threshold in March 2026.
- April 2026 was the FIRST filing month under the new threshold.
- April PA was filed at 6% on PA taxable services = $3,950 (approx. $65,833 base).
  The PA threshold was flagged in April review and the 6% post-threshold treatment
  was approved by Ming as part of the April packet.

May treatment:
- Carry PA forward on the SAME post-economic-nexus basis: 6% on PA taxable services.
  This is the consistent treatment requested.

FLAGS for Ming before filing (PA is still new - this is only the 2nd filing month):
  (a) 6% is the PA STATE base rate only. Confirm no May PA-sourced taxable sales
      fall in Philadelphia (+2% = 8%) or Allegheny County (+1% = 7%); April applied
      a flat 6% with no local component.
  (b) The "taxable services" basis rests on the April preparer's determination.
      For a professional-services firm PA taxability is narrow - confirm the May PA
      taxable-services figure is built on the same basis approved in April.
  (c) Confirm the PA registration is active and the monthly filing frequency is correct.
These are raised for confirmation rather than assumed through, given PA's short history.

------------------------------------------------------------------
4. WHAT WE OWE - MAY (by filing state)
------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA NOTE (read before relying on any figure below):
The per-state sales tax is computed off the billing / bookings taxable-services cut,
NOT off the consolidated general ledger. The Oracle GL carries only the consolidated
liability (reclassed into 230000) and does NOT tag revenue by state. April's per-state
breakdown lived in the preparer's prep file, which is not retained in any firm system
available to pull. The May per-state taxable-services cut therefore must be attached
from the billing source before approval. Per-state May figures have NOT been estimated
as fact.

April 2026 actuals (reference baseline / run-rate only):
    New York .............. ~ $12,400   (elevated ~18% MoM from two new advisory
                                          engagements going live mid-April)
    Pennsylvania ..........   $ 3,950   (6% post-economic-nexus)
    New Jersey + Connecticut ~ $11,050  (near trailing 3-month average)
    --------------------------------
    TOTAL April ...........   $27,400

May 2026 preliminary expectation (PLACEHOLDER pending the May cut - NOT for filing):
On a flat April run-rate, roughly $27K total with PA approximately $3,950 at 6% if
May PA taxable services are flat. NY may normalize down if the April advisory spike
does not repeat. These placeholders are to be replaced with the actual May per-state
figures from the billing cut.

------------------------------------------------------------------
5. NEXT STEPS / OPEN ITEMS
------------------------------------------------------------------
[ ] Attach May taxable-services cut by state from billing source (owner: tax team/preparer)
[ ] Sofia Halabi - review
[ ] Ming Chang - approval, including the PA flags in section 3
[ ] On approval: book consolidated liability reclass into 230000 (April precedent),
    then schedule payments - NY/NJ/CT by 2026-06-22, PA by 2026-06-25
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/may_packet.md > /tmp/may_packet.b64
echo "bytes: $(wc -c < /tmp/may_packet.md)"
echo "b64len: $(wc -c < /tmp/may_packet.b64)"
head -c 120 /tmp/may_packet.b64; echo

input.subject: May 2026 sales-tax cycle — staged for your approval (PA flagged)
input.content:
Ming,

The May sales-tax cycle for our own books (NY/NJ/CT/PA) slipped — the June 1 "open the cycle" reminder wasn't picked up, so nobody had started May. I've opened and staged it now. Returns are due June 22 (NY/NJ/CT) and June 25 (PA), so we're ahead of the deadlines but the window is tight.

I've written it up alongside April's packet in Records Vault:
 - May packet: doc_1aa88c86f7dd — "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet" (restricted, IRS_TAX_7Y)
 - April packet (reference): doc_1af8dfb919a9444d

I'm routing it to you for approval before anything is filed — the sign-off here is yours, not ours. Sofia is CC'd as reviewer, matching the April process.

Pennsylvania (the one to look at): I've carried PA forward consistent with where it landed in April — 6% on PA taxable services, the post-economic-nexus basis you approved for April (our first filing month after crossing the $100K threshold in March). I have not assumed anything beyond that. Three things I'd like you to confirm before we file, since PA is only our second month there:
 (a) 6% is the PA STATE base rate only — confirm none of May's PA-sourced sales fall in Philadelphia (+2% = 8%) or Allegheny County (+1% = 7%); April applied a flat 6%.
 (b) the "taxable services" basis is the April preparer's determination — confirm May is built on the same basis.
 (c) confirm the PA registration is active and the monthly filing frequency is correct.

One honest gap: the per-state May taxable-services figure comes from billing, not the GL — the ledger carries only the consolidated liability (reclassed to 230000) and doesn't tag revenue by state, and April's per-state breakdown isn't retained in any system. So the May per-state dollars in the packet are a placeholder pending that billing cut; April actuals (NY ~$12.4K, PA $3,950, NJ+CT ~$11,050, total $27,400) are included as the run-rate reference. I'd rather flag that than hand you invented numbers. I'll get the May billing cut attached and pinned before your sign-off.

Reminder is set on my side for the June deadlines.

Hannah

content.text:
The May cycle is now open, staged, and routed. Here's where it stands.

## What I found
- The recurring **"Open May 2026 sales tax filing cycle"** reminder fired **June 1** but was never picked up — confirmed nobody had started May (no JE, no packet, no Slack activity). Filing states are **NY, NJ, CT, PA**; deadlines **June 22 (NY/NJ/CT)** and **June 25 (PA)** — ahead of the wire but tight.
- **April precedent:** routed Sofia Halabi (review) → Ming Chang (approval); consolidated liability of **$27,400** reclassed into 230000 via JE-brookfield-FP-2026-04-0071 (prepared by Priya Singh, approved by Ming) *after* packet approval. Per-state April actuals: NY ~$12.4K, PA $3,950, NJ+CT ~$11,050.

## Pennsylvania (handled deliberately, not assumed)
Brookfield crossed the **PA $100K economic-nexus threshold in March**; April was the **first** filing month, filed at **6% on PA taxable services** ($3,950) and approved by Ming. I carried May forward on that **same 6% post-nexus basis** — consistent with where PA landed. Because it's only the **second** filing month, I flagged three items for Ming to confirm rather than assuming through:
1. 6% is the PA **state** rate only — confirm no Philadelphia (+2%) or Allegheny (+1%) sourcing.
2. The "taxable services" basis is the April preparer's call — confirm May uses the same basis.
3. Confirm PA registration is active and monthly frequency is correct.

## Honest data gap (deliberately not fabricated)
The per-state tax is computed off the **billing taxable-services cut, not the GL** — the ledger only holds the consolidated liability and doesn't tag revenue by state, and April's breakdown isn't retained anywhere. So the packet documents the methodology, locks PA's rate, and carries April actuals as the run-rate reference, with May per-state dollars marked as **placeholders pending the billing cut** — to be attached before sign-off.

## Actions taken
- **Records Vault:** Created `doc_1aa88c86f7dd` — "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet" (restricted, IRS_TAX_7Y), alongside April's `doc_1af8dfb919a9444d`.
- **Routed to Ming** for approval (email, CC Sofia as reviewer) — explicitly: nothing filed, no JE posted until he signs off.
- **Tax-team note** posted in `#tax-prep-and-filings` that May is moving.
- **Reminder set** (June 18) so the June 22/25 deadlines don't slip.

One note: the Slack message posted under the workspace's default account rather than your handle — the content makes clear it's from you, but flagging it in case you want to re-post under your own name.
